title: Predication
summary: Covers Intel IA-64 predication: predicate registers, predicated instructions, and how branches are eliminated by executing both paths with conditional commitment.
content_points: Each instruction has an associated predicate (Boolean bit stored in a predicate register).
An instruction produces effects only if its predicate is true; otherwise result is discarded.
Example: if(R1==0){R2=5; R3=8;} else R2=21; → cmp R1,0,P1; <P1>mov R2,5; <P1>mov R3,8; <P2>mov R2,21.
learning_objectives: Explain how predication eliminates branch control flow
Convert a simple if-else to predicated assembly
